texto: Even if you are alive somewhere, the absence of the other person who used to be there beside you obliterates your presence. Everything in the room, even the stars in the sky, can disappear in a second, changing one scene for another, just like in a dream.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Hwang Sok-yong: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “Even if you are alive somewhere, the absence of the other person who used to be there beside you …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, death, death-of-a-loved-one pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e death-of-a-loved-one.

frase_original: Even if you are alive somewhere, the absence of the other person who used to be there beside you obliterates your presence. Everything in the room, even the stars in the sky, can disappear in a second, changing one scene for another, just like in a dream.

texto: There is an old saying that goes 'Start by plucking a hair, end by killing a man'. It is also said, 'Two hands must meet to make a sound'. The atrocities that happened here weren't carried out by strangers - it was us, the people who'd once lived together harmoniously in the same village.""They say it was the superstitious freaks who did it.""No, it was Satan who did it.""Come now, what sort of a ghost is that?"Ryu Yosop replied, "It is the black thing that lives in the heart of every man.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Hwang Sok-yong que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “There is an old saying that goes 'Start by plucking a hair, end by killing a man'. It is also sai…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, death, evil, com foco em luto e termino.

The atrocities that happened here weren't carried out by strangers - it was us, the people who'd once lived together harmoniously in the same village.""They say it was the superstitious freaks who did it.""No, it was Satan who did it.""Come now, what sort of a ghost is that?"Ryu Yosop replied, "It is the black thing that lives in the heart of every man.

texto: The blazing sun beat down on the concrete of the museum's front yard- Reverend Ryu Yosop felt as if the heat were sucking up all the moisture in his brain and heart. What different colors he and his brother Yohan must have used as each of them painted their own picture of home, of the carnage. These people have constructed yet a different vision of their own, Yosop thought to himself, but it all stems from the same nightmare, the one we created together.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Hwang Sok-yong: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “The blazing sun beat down on the concrete of the museum's front yard- Reverend Ryu Yosop felt as …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, korea, korean-war pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

texto: People hated and killed each other back then. Now even those who survived are dying, leaving this world one by one. Unless we find a way to forgive one another, none of us will ever be able to see each other again. (2007: 88)
explicacao: Citação de Hwang Sok-yong sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “People hated and killed each other back then. Now even those who survived are dying, leaving this…” articula reflexao, forgiveness, korean-war em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como korean-war.

texto: The scene [Bruegel's 'Landscape with the Fall of Icarus'] is filled with a vast field, and a cow and a farmer plowing. In the left-hand corner is a tiny ocean the size of a palm, and there, I can barely make it out, the two legs of a man who fell headlong into the sea. This is called the Fall of Icarus. Compared to everyday life, the fall of an idealist who flew too high with candle-wax wings is an unremarkable tragedy.
explicacao: Citação de Hwang Sok-yong sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “The scene [Bruegel's 'Landscape with the Fall of Icarus'] is filled with a vast field, and a cow …” articula reflexao, hwang-sok-yong, idealism em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como idealism e perspective.
